title: Fiery Molten Dutch Morbier Frittata
description: A spicy, molten brunch dish featuring morbier cheese, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This frittata is dairy-free friendly and made with a brine-dried morbier cheese.

ingredients.name: Vegan egg replacement (e.g. tofu or aquafaba)

instructions: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped bell pepper, red onion, and garlic clove. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the spicy sausage and cook for 2-3 minutes, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the brine-dried morbier cheese, smoked paprika, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper. Stir until the cheese is melted and the mixture is well combined.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the vegan egg replacement and a splash of water.
Pour the egg mixture over the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the edges start to set.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, until the frittata is set and the edges are golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Use a spatula to carefully loosen the edges of the frittata from the skillet.
Slide the frittata out of the skillet onto a plate and serve hot.
